perturbation.original_text: This Exhibit B is entered into as of the 26th day of March 2020 by and between Kubient, Inc. ("Kubient"), and The Associated Press ("Customer"). This Exhibit is hereby incorporated into and made a part of the Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") between the Parties (Effective Date: February 5, 2020).
perturbation.changed_text: This Exhibit B is entered into as of the 26th day of March 2020 by and between Kubient, Inc. ("Kubient"), and The Associated Press ("Client"). This Exhibit is hereby incorporated into and made a part of the Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") between the Parties (Effective Date: February 5, 2020).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Customer' is replaced with 'Client'. Throughout the original text, 'Customer' is used. Changing it here creates ambiguity as to whether 'Client' and 'Customer' refer to the same entity. This introduces a contradiction because obligations are placed on 'Customer' elsewhere in the document. This change affects the entire document where 'Customer' is referenced, especially in defining the responsibilities of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SERVICES: Kubient shall provide to Customer advertising and related business revenue consultation with respect to the AP News site, AP News mobile app, AP DNE sites and widgets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SERVICES: Kubient shall provide to Customer advertising and related business revenue consultation with respect to the AP News site, AP News mobile app, AP DNE sites and widgets.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Customer' for The Associated Press, while the changed text replaces 'Customer' with 'Client'. The contradicted text states that "Kubient shall provide to Customer advertising..." By changing 'Customer' to 'Client' in the initial definition, there is now ambiguity as to whether Kubient is obligated to provide services to 'Client' (Associated Press) per the SERVICES section. The services section refers to 'Customer' and the exhibit now refers to 'Client' introducing a strong in-text contradiction regarding to whom Kubient must provide services.

perturbation.original_text: REVENUE SHARE/FEE: Commencing on March 1, 2020 and thereafter, the Parties shall share revenue generated from Customer's consumer offerings, including but not limited to its content, technology, traffic, data, websites, apps, videos and podcasts, without offset and regardless of which Party is responsible for securing such revenue, as per the attached Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: REVENUE SHARE/FEE: Commencing on March 1, 2020 and thereafter, the Parties shall share revenue generated from User's consumer offerings, including but not limited to its content, technology, traffic, data, websites, apps, videos and podcasts, without offset and regardless of which Party is responsible for securing such revenue, as per the attached Schedule 1.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Customer's consumer offerings' is replaced with 'User's consumer offerings'. This introduces a contradiction, because there is no defined "User", making it ambiguous as to whose consumer offerings are subject to the revenue share agreement. This contradicts the implicit understanding that the revenue share is tied to the Customer (The Associated Press).
perturbation.contradicted_location: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship. During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient and compensation to Kubient under the applicable exhibit and/or schedule. Provided however, this provision shall not apply in the event Customer can establish, in writing, that it had a preexisting working relationship with such Third-Party prior to the Agreement and without Kubient's assistance. Customer acknowledges that this provision is reasonable and necessary for the protection of Kubient and that Kubient will be irrevocably damaged if such covenant is not specifically enforced. Customer further agrees that Kubient will be entitled to seek injunctive relief for the purpose of restraining Customer from violating this covenant (and no bond or other security shall be required in connection therewith) in addition to any other relief to which Kubient may be entitled under the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Customer' to 'User' in the revenue share clause does introduce ambiguity, as there is no defined 'User.' However, this ambiguity does not create a direct, strong contradiction with the 'Non-Circumvent' clause. The Non-Circumvent clause focuses on protecting Kubient's third-party relationships with the Customer, and the ambiguity introduced by changing 'Customer' to 'User' does not directly conflict with or invalidate this protection. The Non-Circumvent clause still functions as intended, regardless of the ambiguity surrounding the revenue share agreement. Therefore, the contradiction is not strong enough to warrant a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer shall not, without Kubient's prior written consent, directly or indirectly (i) solicit or encourage any person to leave the employment or other service of Kubient; or (ii) hire, on behalf of Customer or any other person or entity, any person who has left the employment of Kubient within the one (1) year period following the termination or end of that person's employment.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, the Associated Press shall not, without Kubient's prior written consent, directly or indirectly (i) solicit or encourage any person to leave the employment or other service of Kubient; or (ii) hire, on behalf of the Associated Press or any other person or entity, any person who has left the employment of Kubient within the one (1) year period following the termination or end of that person's employment.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Customer' with 'the Associated Press', this is not a semantic change that would cause confusion, but replacing it in ONLY one instance creates an implicit contradiction. Since elsewhere 'Customer' is used, now the contract may be interpreted as implying the non-solicitation applies to only the Associated Press but not to its subsidiaries. This alters the scope of the restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_location: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship. During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient and compensation to Kubient under the applicable exhibit and/or schedule. Provided however, this provision shall not apply in the event Customer can establish, in writing, that it had a preexisting working relationship with such Third-Party prior to the Agreement and without Kubient's assistance. Customer acknowledges that this provision is reasonable and necessary for the protection of Kubient and that Kubient will be irrevocably damaged if such covenant is not specifically enforced. Customer further agrees that Kubient will be entitled to seek injunctive relief for the purpose of restraining Customer from violating this covenant (and no bond or other security shall be required in connection therewith) in addition to any other relief to which Kubient may be entitled under the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Customer shall not...', while the changed text replaces 'Customer' with 'the Associated Press'. The contradicted text refers to 'Customer' in the context of non-circumvention. The change introduces an in-text contradiction because it creates ambiguity as to whether the non-circumvention clause applies to all 'Customers' as defined elsewhere, or specifically only to the Associated Press, potentially excluding its subsidiaries or other entities that would otherwise be considered 'Customer' under the agreement, altering the scope of the non-circumvention obligation. This is a misaligned terminology that leads to conflicting obligations, as described in the Perturbation Definitions.
